name: Ensure Managed IAM Policies are used instead of Inline Policies
body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **managed IAM Policies are used instead of Inline Policies** across AWS.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment focuses on inline Policies used instead of managed IAM Policies. It reviews the identity, credential, role, or policy state that creates the access condition described by the control.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.
description: Inline Policies used instead of managed IAM Policies
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

### Console
1. Locate identities with inline policies.
2. Create equivalent customer-managed policies.
3. Attach managed policies.
4. Remove inline policies.

### AWS CLI
~~~bash
aws iam create-policy --policy-name <policy-name> --policy-document file://policy.json
aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name <role-name> --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:policy/<policy-name>
aws iam delete-role-policy --role-name <role-name> --policy-name <inline-policy-name>
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies_managed-vs-inline.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/iam/
